seoTitle: How to Combine Different Sizes in One Canva File
metaDescription: Work with multiple page sizes inside a single Canva file to design cohesive assets for varied formats and platforms.
intro: Projects often need the same branding across different formats — a square social post, a wide banner, a printed flyer — and managing that inside one file keeps everything consistent. This guide covers combining different page sizes in a single Canva project. It's aimed at designers and marketers producing multi-format campaigns.

steps.heading: Access your Canva file
steps.body: Open the project where you want to add pages of a different size.

steps.heading: Choose different sizes for new pages
steps.body: Add a new page and set its dimensions independently from the rest of the file.

steps.heading: Combine the pages
steps.body: Arrange the varied-size pages within the same file to keep your project organized.

steps.heading: Apply final touches
steps.body: Adjust each page's design to maintain consistent branding despite the different dimensions.

faq.question: Can each page in a Canva file have a completely different size?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva allows individual pages within a project to use different dimensions.

faq.question: Does combining sizes affect how I export the file?
faq.answer: You can typically choose to export all pages or select specific ones, regardless of their differing sizes.

faq.question: Is this useful for a full marketing campaign?
faq.answer: Yes, it helps keep social posts, banners, and print materials consistent within a single organized project.
